skill.name: vercel-deploy-claimable
skill.description: Guide agents through creating a Vercel preview deployment and returning both the live preview URL and the claim URL for account transfer.
skill.summary: Deploy a project to Vercel and return preview and claim links.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Most static matches are false positives from Markdown command examples, inline code, and official Vercel or Claude URLs. The confirmed risk is that the skill instructs agents to run an absolute-path shell script that is not present in the audited package, then package and upload project contents to Vercel. This is not confirmed malicious, but it is unsafe to publish until the executable script is included and reviewed.

high_findings.title: Unaudited Absolute-Path Shell Script Execution
high_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to execute bash /mnt/skills/user/vercel-deploy/scripts/deploy.sh, but the audited package contains only SKILL.md. Running an external absolute-path script could execute unreviewed code and bypass marketplace review.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and targets a shell script outside the audited file tree. The repository content available for review does not include that script, so the executed behavior cannot be verified.

medium_findings.title: Project Content Upload to External Deployment Service
medium_findings.description: The skill describes packaging the target project into a tarball and uploading it to a deployment service. This is expected for Vercel deployment, but it can expose source code, assets, and configuration files if users do not review what is being packaged.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upload workflow is described directly and the URLs show the deployment result. The risk is contextual because deployment requires network transfer, but the destination appears to be Vercel rather than an unknown endpoint.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Official Service URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the upstream Vercel skill source, Vercel preview and claim links, and Claude network settings. These are expected for the documented deployment workflow and do not show credential exfiltration by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains are visible in the documentation and match the deployment service or host tool settings. No evidence found that these URLs receive secrets outside the stated deployment flow.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts appear to be keyword matches in descriptive text, not executable code. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, reconnaissance commands, or prompt injection instructions in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language metadata or framework lists. They do not contain executable cryptographic code, host discovery commands, or instruction override text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides bash commands that an agent may run automatically during deployment. This pattern requires close review because command execution can modify files, access local project data, and run arbitrary script contents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bash invocation is explicit and repeated in the usage examples. The exact script implementation is not available in the audited package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Packaging Before Network Upload
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow packages a project directory or tarball and uploads it to a deployment service. This can be legitimate, but it should exclude secrets, private source, build artifacts, and unrelated local files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package-and-upload behavior is stated in the skill documentation. The risk depends on what the missing deployment script actually includes or excludes.

content.value_statement: Teams often need a quick preview link without setting up a full Vercel project first. This skill describes a claimable Vercel deployment flow that returns both a live preview URL and a claim URL.
actual_capabilities: Documents when to use a Vercel deployment workflow for preview or production requests.
Shows the shell command format for deploying a directory or existing tarball.
Describes framework detection from package.json for common frontend and backend frameworks.
Explains the expected preview URL and claim URL returned after deployment.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for Claude network egress restrictions.
limitations: The referenced deployment script is not included in the audited package.
Deployment requires network access to Vercel domains.
Packaging project files can expose source code or sensitive files if exclusions are incomplete.
The skill does not describe authentication, rollback, environment variables, or deployment logs.

use_cases.title: Share a Preview Build
use_cases.description: Create a temporary Vercel preview link for a web app so reviewers can inspect the latest work.

use_cases.title: Hand Off a Claimable Deployment
use_cases.description: Produce a Vercel claim URL so a teammate can transfer the deployment into their own Vercel account.

use_cases.title: Test Static Site Hosting
use_cases.description: Deploy a simple HTML project and confirm that the root page is served correctly.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy Current Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy the current project to Vercel and show me the preview URL and claim URL.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a default deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a Specific Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy the web app in the selected project folder to Vercel. Confirm the framework you detect before deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has more than one project directory.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Review Link
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Vercel preview deployment for this branch and summarize any files that should be excluded before upload.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewer needs a live link and packaging visibility.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Deployment Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the package contents, deploy only the intended app directory to Vercel, then provide the preview link, claim link, and any deployment warnings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with security and scope checks.

output_examples.input: Deploy this app and give me the link.
output_examples.output: Deployment completed. The response includes a live Vercel preview link and a claim link for transferring the deployment.

output_examples.input: Create a preview deployment for the static site.
output_examples.output: The static site is prepared for root serving, deployed to Vercel, and presented with both preview and claim links.

output_examples.input: Vercel deployment failed because network access is blocked.
output_examples.output: The response explains that Vercel domains must be allowed in Claude network settings before retrying the deployment.

best_practices: Review packaged files before deployment and remove secrets, private data, and unrelated directories.
Use the narrowest project path possible instead of deploying an entire workspace.
Verify the preview URL before sharing the claim URL with another person.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy repositories that contain production secrets or private customer data.
Do not run an external deployment script unless its source has been reviewed.
Do not treat a claimable preview deployment as a replacement for production release controls.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Vercel account?
faq.answer: The documented flow says no authentication is required for the preview deployment. A claim URL is used to transfer it later.

faq.question: What files are excluded from the deployment package?
faq.answer: The documentation says node_modules and .git are excluded. Other exclusions are not described.

faq.question: Can it deploy static HTML?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes special handling for projects without package.json and for single HTML files.

faq.question: Which frameworks are mentioned?
faq.answer: The documentation mentions Next.js, Gatsby, Remix, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Astro, Angular, Express, Vite, and other common frameworks.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The audited package does not include the deployment script that the skill asks the agent to run.

faq.question: What should users check before using it?
faq.answer: Users should inspect the deployment script, confirm the upload destination, and remove sensitive files before deployment.
